intro: Gymnogramma dealbata is a plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions, commonly known as the "Devil's Claw". The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and analgesic properties, providing relief from pain, arthritis, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma dealbata include harpagoside, a unique iridoid glycoside, as well as flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gymnogramma dealbata is often prepared as a tincture, capsule, or decoction, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including back pain, joint inflammation, and digestive disorders.
